context: This Chinese stamp was created as a temporary solution when high-value stamps were needed urgently after the official opening of the postal service in 1896. It uses unused red print paper stored by the printing office to create different face values of stamps, which were then sold and used temporarily until regular stamps could be produced.
visual_features: Red color, intricate design, text in both Chinese characters and Roman numerals, "REVENUE" label on bottom right corner.

context: "元宵節" is also known as "上元節", "小正月", "元夕", etc. It is celebrated on the fifteenth day of the first lunar month every year. In Hong Kong, it is called "Loi Shing Tong". This festival has been passed down through generations and is still widely observed today.
visual_features: Lanterns, red ribbons, Chinese characters, temple entrance, festive decorations.

metadata.category_description: Encompasses cultural festivals, holidays, and ceremonies, along with the associated customs, rituals, and practices. Examples include events like Chinese New Year, Diwali, and Christmas, each rich in traditions, foods, and rituals that symbolize community and heritage.
